subject: Gaming
title: The Power of Agency
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Power of Agency usable.

hook.text: Games give you control in a world that often doesn't. That feeling of agency is psychologically powerful.

buildup.text: Psychologists found that perceived control reduces stress, even when the control is partially an illusion.

discovery.text: Open-world games let players choose what to do, when, and how. That freedom satisfies deep psychological needs.

twist.text: Even linear games create the illusion of choice. Dialogue options that don't change outcomes still feel good.

climax.text: Agency is why gaming reduces anxiety for many people. Control in a virtual world relieves real-world stress.

punchline.text: You can't control life. But in a game, every choice is yours.

quiz.question: Why is the feeling of agency important in games?
options: Perceived control reduces stress and satisfies deep psychological needs
